ArchitectureNotes.objective: Multi-user Telegram → Claude Code CLI WIKI assistant (Karpathy LLM Wiki method). MVP delivers: foundation (settings/logging/three-DB SQLite), auth + onboarding + admin, scheduler core, Stage-0 Haiku classifier, Stage-1 Sonnet wiki runner + lifecycle, inbox materialise + idempotency, ops (PII/retention, backup/git), Telegram I/O text layer + voice STT + photo ingest, ingest pipeline (classifier + streaming), runtime wiring, integration E2E.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: No i18n catalog (ru-only, D-032); off-site backup deferred; web UI / non-Telegram channels out of scope.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: users.toml drift between SSoT file and sessions.db cache — mitigated by SIGHUP+watchdog reload + sha256 short-circuit (D-031).
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: SQLite three-DB topology requires per-engine PRAGMAs; missed PRAGMA = silent corruption — mitigated by shared apply_sqlite_pragmas listener.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-3: Claude CLI subprocess invocation drift between Stage-0 and Stage-1 (system prompt, cwd, stdin, --verbose) — mitigated by shared M-CLAUDE-CLI-COMMON primitives + integration test against the real CLI.

Phase-0.NAME: Foundation scaffold
Phase-0.description: Repo skeleton, pyproject + uv lock, ruff/mypy, pre-commit, settings, structured logging, three-DB engines + ORM models, users.toml allowlist with SIGHUP/watchdog hot-reload.

Phase-4.NAME: Scheduler core
Phase-4.description: APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler factory (D-003), 5-lane priority queue, 3-tier WIKI lock manager, failure taxonomy + 3-strikes auto-disable (D-019), DLQ insert, SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL kill sequence (D-021), periodic maintenance-job registration.

Phase-5.NAME: Stage-0 classifier
Phase-5.description: Stage-0 Haiku intent classifier: prompt cache (semver+sha256), backend abstraction (Claude CLI / Anthropic API / Fake), Pydantic intent schema, NL time parser (dateparser → Haiku-fallback → escalate). Hardened by aisw-p5b (CLI envelope) and aisw-d3i (CLI alignment).

Phase-6.NAME: Inbox-WIKI materialise + hint cache
Phase-6.description: Per-user Inbox-WIKI/ first-contact materialise, "## Inbox hint" parser + sessions.inbox_hint_cache repo (stat→cache-hit hot path), media staging+promotion (D-004, D-016, D-022). Extended by aisw-8r9 (promote-on-success + 24h sweep).

Phase-7.NAME: Wiki Stage-1 runner
Phase-7.description: Stage-1a/1b Sonnet runner: assemble prompt (base+overlay+per-WIKI CLAUDE.md), acquire WIKI lock, spawn `claude --output-format stream-json --verbose`, parse stream events, hard timeout + kill-sequence, aggregate assistant text. Hardened by aisw-d3i/aisw-kpb/aisw-w83; aisw-m2m (media paths) + aisw-t0n (per-call vision timeout).

Phase-8.NAME: Wiki lifecycle
Phase-8.description: WIKI name normalisation (Cyrillic→Latin ISO 9, PascalCase), owner-scoped create/lookup/soft-delete/restore with anti-spam cap + near-duplicate detection, CLAUDE.md frontmatter + managed/user-zone markers + template loader, 5-step pre-flight grounding (D-008, D-039, D-041).

Phase-9.NAME: Ingest idempotency
Phase-9.description: Two-layer ingest dedup (D-018): L1 tg_updates (24h) + L2 seen_files (SHA-256, 30d) with NFKC text normalisation; records dedup outcomes into audit.db.

Phase-10.NAME: TG text layer
Phase-10.description: aiogram bot + allowlist outer-middleware (D-031), graduated 3-tier confirmations + 10min TTL (D-023), output-size hybrid policy + persistence (D-024, D-025), HTML-balanced throttled streaming edits (D-026).

Phase-11.NAME: TG media — voice + photo
Phase-11.description: Voice (ogg/opus) faster-whisper CPU STT + staging hand-off; photo ingestion → MediaRef → Claude vision via the wiki pipeline; staging→raw promotion (D-022). audio + video_note route to the voice path (aisw-hcl).

Phase-12.NAME: Onboarding + admin
Phase-12.description: Unknown-telegram_id onboarding (pending_users CRUD + intro template + slug lint), admin surface (approve/reject pending, /admin elevate + 30min token, demote, shadow channel), TENANCY_MODE (D-028, D-030, D-031, D-032).

Phase-13.NAME: Ops — PII + retention + GDPR
Phase-13.description: Tiered PII redactor (NIST SP 800-122) for structlog + DB writes, §10.4 retention purge jobs + _trash/_staging sweeps, GDPR purge_user admin endpoint with allow-listed stores (D-034 §10.4). Wired into __main__ by aisw-7k0.

Phase-14.NAME: Ops — backup + per-WIKI git
Phase-14.description: Daily db_snapshot (VACUUM INTO state/snapshots/<UTC>/, 7d rolling retention), per-WIKI local git auto-commit helpers, restore runbook (D-037, tech-spec §10.2/§10.5).

Phase-18.NAME: Runtime wiring
Phase-18.description: Process entrypoint (__main__.py) composing existing modules into a runnable bot: Settings → per-DB alembic upgrade → engines → allowlist sync → APScheduler → aiogram polling → graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown. Enables `uv run python -m ai_steward_wiki` locally with AISW_TG_BOT_TOKEN_LOCAL. No production handlers yet; unrouted updates silently ignored (separate future chunk).

Phase-19.NAME: TG handlers wiring
Phase-19.description: Compose aiogram Router with 5 handlers (text/voice/photo/document/confirm callback) wired into Dispatcher via build_dispatcher(pipeline=...). DefaultPipeline coordinates L1 idempotency dedup, optional voice/photo staging, ack delivery via TgSender, and confirmation callback resolution. Building blocks unchanged; this chunk is composition + e2e tests.

Phase-20.NAME: TG pipeline classifier wiring
Phase-20.description: Replace ack-only DefaultPipeline.on_text/on_voice with Stage-0 Haiku Classifier → Inbox.stage (L2 dedup) → WikiRunner (Stage-1a/1b Sonnet) → deliver_output. Closes the core MVP-launch gap. SSoT: docs/superpowers/plans/20260511-ai-steward-wiki-launch/breakdown.xml chunk-20. bd_id materializes via bd create at chunk start.

Phase-21.NAME: TG pipeline streaming
Phase-21.description: Wrap WikiRunner with StreamEditor (1.5s throttle, Δ50, chain-split 4000) when estimated_duration_s > 5 (DEC-L2). Placeholder edit-in-place + final flush guarantee. SSoT: launch breakdown chunk-21.

Phase-22.NAME: TG document full handler
Phase-22.description: Mime-routing in on_document (DEC-L3): pdf→OCR, text/*→inline, image/*→photo path, else reject. L2 dedup on doc_sha256. Filename PII tier-2 hash. SSoT: launch breakdown chunk-22.

Phase-23.NAME: Integration E2E suite
Phase-23.description: tests/integration/test_e2e_pipeline.py against real Claude CLI + FakeAiogramBot. 4 scenarios: text, voice, photo+confirm, pdf. make integration target. SSoT: launch breakdown chunk-23. Last gate before production cutover (see cutover-checklist.md).

Phase-A.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-A — stage raw content + Router-Claude invocation
Phase-A.description: First slice of smart-inbox-routing (epic aisw-t2r). New inbox/router.py (RouterIntent, RouterDecision, RouterError, parse_router_reply); prompts/inbox.md tightened to a fenced ```router block (v1.1.0); DefaultPipeline gains an optional Router — routable intents (WIKI_INGEST/WIKI_QUERY/UNKNOWN) go through router.route(), the bot replies with RouterDecision.notes; _RouterAdapter in __main__ (ensure_inbox_wiki → stage raw payload into Inbox-WIKI/raw/ → run_wiki_session in Inbox-WIKI/ with prompts/inbox.md → parse_router_reply; WikiRunnerError→RouterError). Domain-WIKI move + Stage-1b ingest + confirm loop + cron bridge + hint fast-path are later phases (aisw-zd9/e45/kcz/12t). NOTE: do not deploy master with Phase-A until aisw-zd9 closes. Discovery/design/plan: docs/superpowers/specs|plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-router-*.md.

Phase-B2.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-B — RouterDecision → domain WIKI select/materialise + move + Stage-1b ingest
Phase-B2.description: Closes the Phase-A loop (epic aisw-t2r). New inbox/route.py (RouteTarget/RouteRejection/StagedRaw; resolve_target_wiki — lookup-or-create the target <Domain>-WIKI, ROUTE-missing → create + warning, AntiSpamCapError/WikiNameError → rejection; stage_raw_into_wiki — write raw/<ts>_<source>.md + promote media via promote_path_to_raw; pick_domain_overlay; build_ingest_prompt). DefaultPipeline gains an optional Librarian + IngestOutcome — a ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI RouterDecision (with a wired librarian + output) → librarian.ingest() → reply notes + Stage-1b summary via OutputDelivery (ok) or send_message (rejected/run_failed); CLARIFY/REJECT and librarian=None keep Phase-A behaviour. _LibrarianAdapter in __main__ (resolve target → stage raw → run_wiki_session in <Domain>-WIKI/ with prompts/wiki.md + domain overlay, timeout_s=None → IngestOutcome). Confirm loop + cron bridge + hint fast-path remain later (aisw-e45/kcz/12t). With Phase-A+B done the "do not deploy master" caveat is lifted. Discovery/design/plan: docs/superpowers/specs|plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-route-ingest-*.md.

Phase-C.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-C — route confirm loop (propose via inline buttons, execute on confirm)
Phase-C.description: Adds a one-tap user confirmation between the Stage-1a router decision and the Stage-1b move+ingest (epic aisw-t2r). DefaultPipeline._run_text_pipeline no longer ingests a ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI RouterDecision immediately — it builds a route_ingest confirm draft (RouterDecision + user_text + source + media_paths + correlation_id, via inbox.route.route_action_to_payload) and proposes it through ConfirmationService.request_explicit with a 2-button keyboard (tg.confirm.build_route_confirm_keyboard); on_confirm_callback dispatches route_ingest pending rows to _handle_route_confirm — resolve race-safely, on "confirmed" replay the decision through Librarian.ingest (the Phase-B path) + deliver the reply, else send a ru cancelled/stale notice; cancel/TTL leaves the staged raw in Inbox (D-022). Reuses pending_confirms (no migration); no proposed_actions field on RouterDecision; no toggle (replace, not flag). New inbox/route.py RouteAction + route_action_to/from_payload; new tg/confirm.py build_route_confirm_keyboard + request_explicit keyboard_factory; new tg/pipeline.py ROUTE_CONFIRM_* constants + build_route_recap + _handle_route_confirm; new anchors tg.pipeline.route.confirm_requested|confirm_executed|confirm_cancelled|confirm_stale + tg.pipeline.confirm.route_dispatched. ADR-005. Cron bridge + hint fast-path remain later (aisw-kcz/12t). Discovery/design/plan: docs/superpowers/specs|plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-route-confirm-*.md.

Phase-D.a.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-D.a — reminder_job: Stage-0 fast-path → confirm → APScheduler DateTrigger → TG deliver
Phase-D.a.description: Cron bridge for one-shot reminders (epic aisw-t2r). DefaultPipeline._run_text_pipeline gains a reminder fast-path BEFORE the Stage-1a router: ClassifierResult.intent==REMINDER & confidence≥THRESHOLD → parse_time(text, prefer_future=True, user_tz from UserRecord.tz | Settings.default_user_tz) → on escalate→ru clarification, on explicitly-past absolute date→ru rejection, on recurring-digest phrasing→ru "not yet" (→aisw-19o), else build a category='reminder' confirm draft (when_utc, message=distilled_payload['reminder_text']|raw, lead_time_min) + recap and ConfirmationService.request_explicit(..., keyboard_factory=build_route_confirm_keyboard); on_confirm_callback dispatches reminder rows to _handle_reminder_confirm → on 'confirmed': scheduler.firing.create_reminder_job (INSERT jobs.Job(kind='reminder_job', status='pending', scheduled_at_utc, payload=ReminderPayload); commit; scheduler.add_job(firing.fire_job, DateTrigger(when_utc, UTC), args=[job_id], id='reminder:<id>', misfire_grace_time=None)) + ack; cancel/stale → ru notice. New scheduler/firing.py (set_firing_context/create_reminder_job/fire_job — picklable int args, module-level (TgSender, jobs sessionmaker) registry; fire_job guards status, sends '🔔 Напоминание: …', marks done/failed). Extend storage/jobs/payloads.py (+ReminderPayload), classifier/time_parse.py (+prefer_future), settings.py (+default_user_tz='Europe/Moscow'), __main__.py (firing.set_firing_context after scheduler.start; add_listener(_on_job_missed, EVENT_JOB_MISSED); pass jobs sessionmaker + scheduler into DefaultPipeline). No new dep, no new table, no new migration. New anchors tg.pipeline.reminder.* + scheduler.reminder.*. ADR-006. digest_job/recurring is aisw-19o (Phase-D.b). Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260512-inbox-wiki-cron-bridge-{discovery,design}.md; plan: docs/superpowers/plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-cron-bridge-plan.md.

Phase-D.b.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-D.b — digest_job: recurring aggregator (cron) → Claude with --add-dir into relevant WIKIs
Phase-D.b.description: Umbrella (epic aisw-t2r) — split out of aisw-kcz, then re-split (too large for one context window per Plan Sizing) into Phase-D.b.1 (aisw-oqq) + Phase-D.b.2 (aisw-w3k). The asyncio.PriorityQueue worker-loop consumer (tech-spec §3 producer/consumer) the original description bundled in is DE-SCOPED out of aisw-19o (YAGNI — no consumer today; digest_job MVP direct-fires under Semaphore+WikiLockManager like maintenance jobs already do) → its own future bd issue. Done when aisw-oqq + aisw-w3k close.

Phase-D.b.1.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-D.b.1 — digest_job vertical slice: NL recurrence parsing → CronTrigger, DigestPayload widening, create_digest_job/fire_digest_job (direct-fire), pipeline confirm flow
Phase-D.b.1.description: Runnable vertical slice (epic aisw-t2r). (1) New classifier/recurrence.py: Recurrence value (kind daily|weekly, time_hhmm, weekdays tuple, tz) + to_cron() → APScheduler CronTrigger kwargs; parse_recurrence — conservative rule-based ru regex (daily / weekly-by-weekdays) + optional Haiku fallback (time_parse-shaped) → RecurrenceParseResult; escalate on monthly/interval/raw-cron/ambiguous. (2) storage/jobs/payloads.py: DigestPayload widened — wiki_scope:Literal['all']='all', recurrence:Recurrence, window_hours:int=Field(24, ge=1, le=168), prompt_hint:str|None; extra='forbid'+frozen kept; no Alembic migration. (3) scheduler/firing.py: set_digest_context (module-level digest registry: runner adapter, Semaphore, WikiLockManager, owner-WIKI-set resolver, jobs sessionmaker, TgSender), create_digest_job (INSERT+commit jobs.Job(kind='digest_job', status='scheduled', priority=Lane.DIGEST, payload=DigestPayload); commit-before-add_job; scheduler.add_job(fire_digest_job, CronTrigger(**rec.to_cron(), timezone=rec.tz), args=[job_id], id='digest:<id>', replace_existing=True)), fire_digest_job (picklable int: load Job, guard status=='scheduled'; resolve owner WIKI set minus Inbox-WIKI → primary + extra_add_dirs; assemble prompts/digest.md + jobs.db planner window context; run_wiki_session under Semaphore+WikiLockManager(primary), timeout_s=600; deliver assistant text or ru empty-digest line via tg/output ChainSplitter/send_document; success → status stays 'scheduled', retry_count=0; failure/timeout → retry_count++ and at 3 → status='disabled' + scheduler.remove_job + move_to_dlq (D-019; timeout=Transient strike); bad payload → 'disabled'+DLQ), DigestNotInitialisedError. (4) wiki/runner.py: run_wiki_session(..., extra_add_dirs:list[Path]|None) → appended in _build_argv after the primary --add-dir, next to media_dirs. (5) tg/pipeline.py: _handle_digest_intent (replaces the reminder branch's recurring-keyword stub: parse_recurrence → escalate→ru clarification, else category='digest' PendingConfirmDraft + build_digest_recap + ConfirmationService.request_explicit w/ build_route_confirm_keyboard), _handle_digest_confirm (on_confirm_callback category=='digest' → resolve race-safely → confirmed → create_digest_job + ru ack; cancel/stale → ru notice), ru constants DIGEST_RECAP_RU/_ACK_RU/_UNPARSEABLE_RU/_CONFIRM_CANCELLED_RU/_CONFIRM_STALE_RU/_EMPTY_RU, new DefaultPipeline kwarg(s) for the recurrence parser. (6) __main__.py: firing.set_digest_context(...) after sender/scheduler/runner-adapter; _RecurrenceParserAdapter (wraps parse_recurrence + Stage-0 backend + prompts/recurrence.md if present); _resolve_owner_wikis bound into set_digest_context; pass new pipeline kwargs. (7) New prompts/digest.md (scan-friendly ru summary, semver: line required) + optional prompts/recurrence.md. (8) GRACE: M-CLASSIFIER-RECURRENCE node + V-M-CLASSIFIER-RECURRENCE; M-SCHEDULER-FIRING / M-STORAGE-JOBS / M-WIKI-RUNNER / M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER / M-RUNTIME-WIRING annotations + CrossLinks; V-M-SCHEDULER-FIRING extended; this entry. ADR-007 (digest_job direct-fire vs PriorityQueue; recurrence representation). New anchors classifier.recurrence.parse + tg.pipeline.digest.* + tg.pipeline.confirm.digest_dispatched + scheduler.digest.*. Depends on aisw-kcz. Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260512-inbox-wiki-digest-job-{discovery,design}.md; plan: docs/superpowers/plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-digest-job-plan.md.

Phase-D.b.2a.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-D.b.2a — digest presentation core: deliver_output(kind='digest') D-025 hybrid + run_outputs audit, <b>-section split (n/m) + send_document fallthrough, prompts/digest.md D-024 contract (TL;DR section + sections), real jobs.db planner-window query
Phase-D.b.2a.description: First half of the Phase-D.b.2 split (epic aisw-t2r; too large for one context window → split at the Discovery gate into 2a presentation core + 2b interactive). fire_digest_job now delivers via tg.output.deliver_output(kind='digest') — D-025 size hybrid (≤3500 inline / ≤10000 -header chain-split with (i/M) footers / >10000 LengthCapSummarizer + send_document), full text persisted to /data/runs//.md, audit.run_outputs row (run_id=f'digest-{uuid4().hex[:12]}'); empty-WIKI line stays a plain send_message; _DIGEST_TG_LIMIT truncation removed. set_digest_context +audit_session_maker (6-tuple). _build_planner_context (module-private in firing.py): jobs.Job rows status=='scheduled' AND scheduled_at_utc IS NOT NULL AND ≤ now+window → «- HH:MM — title» in the owner tz; recurring (scheduled_at_utc IS NULL) excluded. prompts/digest.md rewritten to the D-024 contract (semver 0.1.0): 📌 TL;DR first then conditional 📅 Сегодня / 💊 Лекарства / 📈 Трекеры / 📝 Обновления WIKI; HTML whitelist; empty ⇒ «🌿 Сегодня дел нет.». FR-2 (section split + (n/m) + send_document fallthrough) needed no production code — ChainSplitter/deliver_output already implement it. GRACE: M-SCHEDULER-FIRING 0.2.0→0.3.0 (DEPENDS +tg.output.deliver_output), M-RUNTIME-WIRING v0.5.0→v0.5.1; new anchor scheduler.digest.planner_context; scheduler.digest.delivered +run_id/n_messages/document_sent. ADR-024. FR-5..9 (cards / /expand / /digest_now / per-user toggles / named-subset WIKI) → aisw-269 (Phase-D.b.2b). Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260512-inbox-wiki-digest-presentation-{discovery,design}.md; plan: docs/superpowers/plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-digest-presentation.md.

Phase-D.b.2b.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-D.b.2b — digest interactive surface: /digest_now (first slash-command router; reuses fire_digest_job), /expand <section> (scoped Claude re-run via DigestRunner(section=…) + prompts/digest_expand.md), named-subset WIKI selection (DigestPayload.wiki_scope 'all'|list[str] + heuristic extraction in the digest fast-path)
Phase-D.b.2b.description: Second half of the Phase-D.b.2 split (epic aisw-t2r), re-scoped at the Q&A gate (2026-05-12) from the parent's FR-5..9 down to 3 FR: actionable cards and per-user section toggles DEFERRED to their own bds (cards — no data model: reminder_job has no medication/event subtype or done-state, pending_confirm has a 10-min TTL so a cron digest essentially never finds one live; toggles — a toggle table with no flip surface is inert + the flip UX and the first sessions.db migration past baseline want their own pass). Scope: (1) /digest_now — the bot's first aiogram Command-filter handler + a slash-command router registered BEFORE the F.text catch-all (ordinary text still falls through to MessagePipeline unchanged); selects the owner's enabled digest_job rows and runs the existing fire_digest_job(job_id) per row (reuses set_digest_context, the per-WIKI LockAcquirer inside run_wiki_session, deliver_output, planner-window query, 3-strike); 0 jobs → ru hint; per-job exception caught so the rest still run; NO _run_digest core refactor, NO new lock, NO ephemeral-digest path. (2) /expand <section> — keys today|meds|trackers|wiki mirroring the four D-024 <b>-headers; resolves the owner WIKI set, re-runs Claude via a generalised DigestRunner(*, …, section:str|None=None) — None ⇒ prompts/digest.md (byte-identical to today), key ⇒ new prompts/digest_expand.md (section name substituted into user_input); reply via sender.send_message (no run_outputs row — on-demand command reply, not a cron output); unknown/missing key → ru usage; «nothing for this section over the period» is a normal reply. (3) Named-subset WIKI — DigestPayload.wiki_scope: Literal['all'] → 'all' | list[str] (non-empty); VERSION 0.0.4→0.0.5; no jobs.db migration ('all' stays valid; wiki_scope is not the discriminator); create_digest_job wiki_scope:str|list[str]; digest fast-path _extract_wiki_names(text, owner_wiki_stems) — tokens ∩ the owner's *-WIKI/ dir-stems (case-insensitive, whole-token), resolved ⇒ list[str], none ⇒ 'all', a name-shaped-but-unresolved token ⇒ ru clarification + NO job created; fire_digest_job after resolving the owner WIKI set intersects with payload.wiki_scope when it is a list (log scheduler.digest.scope_filter requested/kept/vanished; empty kept ⇒ ru notice + log empty='scope_vanished', no strike, status stays 'scheduled'); the digest recap/ack names the chosen WIKIs. (4) GRACE: updated MODULE_CONTRACTs M-SCHEDULER-FIRING (DigestRunner +section, fire_digest_job scope filter, create_digest_job wiki_scope) / M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING (digest_now + expand command handlers) / M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER (digest fast-path wiki_scope extraction + recap) / M-STORAGE-JOBS (DigestPayload.wiki_scope) / M-RUNTIME-WIRING (_DigestRunnerAdapter section mode + digest_expand prompt path); new prompts/digest_expand.md node; V-M-SCHEDULER-FIRING / V-M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING / V-M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER / V-M-STORAGE-JOBS extended; this entry. ADR-025 (digest interactive surface — slash-command router, /expand scoped re-run, /digest_now reuse-not-fork, wiki_scope widening, AND the explicit cards + toggles deferrals with rationale). New anchors tg.command.digest_now(.empty/.job_failed/.done) + tg.command.expand(.bad_section/.delivered/.failed) + scheduler.digest.scope_filter + tg.pipeline.digest.wiki_unknown. No new dependency; no new SQLite table / Alembic migration. Depends on aisw-w3k; blocks aisw-19o. Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260512-inbox-wiki-digest-interactive-{discovery,design}.md; plan: docs/superpowers/plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-digest-interactive-plan.md.

Phase-D.b.2c.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-D.b.2c — per-user digest section toggles: user_digest_prefs (sessions.db) + alembic/sessions/0002 (first incremental migration past baseline) + /digest_sections inline-toggle command + fire_digest_job directive injection (byte-identical default)
Phase-D.b.2c.description: Deferred out of aisw-269 at the Q&A gate (epic aisw-t2r; ADR-025 §8). (1) New ORM model UserDigestPrefs in storage/sessions/models.py — one row per owner: user_id (PK, FK users.user_id ON DELETE CASCADE), trackers_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, wiki_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, updated_at_utc; absent row ⇒ all on (opt-out feature). New repo module storage/sessions/digest_prefs.py — DigestPrefs frozen dataclass (+disabled_keys), TOGGLEABLE_DIGEST_SECTIONS=('trackers','wiki') (subset of EXPAND_SECTION_KEYS) + SECTION_DISPLAY_NAME (key→ru label w/ emoji), get_digest_prefs (telegram_id→DigestPrefs; defaults if no users/prefs row), set_digest_section (upsert one bool; no-op+defaults if no users row; ValueError on unknown section). (2) alembic/sessions/versions/0002_user_digest_prefs.py — first incremental sessions.db migration past 0001_sessions_baseline; upgrade()=Base.metadata.create_all(bind=op.get_bind()) (IDEMPOTENT — the baseline's create_all of live metadata already builds the table on a fresh DB, so 0002 only does work on an already-baselined existing DB), downgrade()=op.drop_table('user_digest_prefs'); convention recorded in ADR-026 (add-table migrations → model in models.py + a new NNNN_* whose upgrade() calls create_all + downgrade() drops the added table(s); column ALTERs → explicit op.* batch ops). No jobs.db/audit.db change. (3) scheduler/firing.py — set_digest_context +sessions_session_maker (7-tuple _digest_ctx); get_owner_digest_prefs / set_owner_digest_section accessors (read the sessions sessionmaker from the digest context; DigestNotInitialisedError if unwired); fire_digest_job, after building the planner context, reads the owner's user_digest_prefs (try/except → scheduler.digest.prefs_read_failed + DigestPrefs(True,True) degrade-to-all-on) and when a section is off appends «Не включай разделы: <ru labels>.» to the planner context + logs scheduler.digest.sections_filtered — byte-identical otherwise; runner(..., section=None), the DigestRunner Protocol, _DigestRunnerAdapter and run_section_expand untouched (/expand ignores the toggle — explicit request wins). (4) tg/handlers.py — DIGESTSEC_CALLBACK_PREFIX='digestsec:', parse_digestsec_callback (digestsec:<section>:<0|1> → (section, target_enabled)|None — the flag is the TARGET state, idempotent on a stale message), _build_digest_sections_kb (one button per toggleable key), Command('digest_sections') handler (→ firing.get_owner_digest_prefs → ru header + keyboard; tg.command.digest_sections.shown; DigestNotInitialisedError → ru unavailable) + the @router.callback_query(F.data startswith 'digestsec:') handler (parse → firing.set_owner_digest_section → cb.message.edit_reply_markup (TelegramBadRequest 'not modified' swallowed → tg.command.digest_sections.edit_skipped) → cb.answer → tg.command.digest_sections.toggled; bad data → cb.answer + tg.command.digest_sections.bad_callback, no write), both wrapped so nothing bubbles to the dispatcher; registered in the same commands group as /digest_now,/expand (router-order regression still holds). (5) __main__.py — sessions_session_maker passed into firing.set_digest_context. (6) prompts/digest.md semver 0.1.0→0.1.1 — one sentence: «Если в сообщении есть строка вида „Не включай разделы: …" — полностью пропусти перечисленные секции…». (7) GRACE: M-STORAGE-SESSIONS (UserDigestPrefs/DigestPrefs/TOGGLEABLE_DIGEST_SECTIONS/SECTION_DISPLAY_NAME/get_digest_prefs/set_digest_section) / M-SCHEDULER-FIRING (+M-STORAGE-SESSIONS dep; set_digest_context sessions maker; fire_digest_job directive; get/set_owner_digest_section) / M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING (+M-STORAGE-SESSIONS dep; /digest_sections; parse_digestsec_callback; DIGESTSEC_CALLBACK_PREFIX) annotations + CrossLinks (M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING→M-STORAGE-SESSIONS, M-SCHEDULER-FIRING→M-STORAGE-SESSIONS); V-M-STORAGE-SESSIONS / V-M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING / V-M-SCHEDULER-FIRING extended; this entry. ADR-026 (per-user digest section toggles — storage shape, FK target, migration convention, /digest_sections inline-keyboard UX, prompt-injection mechanism, /expand-override decision; cards still deferred). New anchors tg.command.digest_sections.{shown,toggled,bad_callback,edit_skipped,unavailable,error} + scheduler.digest.{sections_filtered,prefs_read_failed}. No new dependency; exactly one new SQLite table + one new Alembic migration, sessions.db only. Out of scope: scheduling-time section selection (DigestPayload untouched), per-WIKI-per-section toggles, a `meds` toggle, actionable inline cards (still deferred per ADR-025 §8). Depends on aisw-269. Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260512-inbox-wiki-digest-section-toggles-{discovery,design}.md; plan: docs/superpowers/plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-digest-section-toggles-plan.md.

Phase-E.cron-add.NAME: aisw-02v — /cron_add + queue consumer (walking skeleton): vertical slice making PriorityJobQueue load-bearing — /cron_add Command handler → M-SCHEDULER-CRON-USER (CronTrigger producer) → PriorityJobQueue(lane=CRON_WRITE) → M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER (single async drain loop, systemd-run --scope --collect wrapped Claude CLI, 600s timeout, bot.send_message delivery via ChainSplitter)
Phase-E.cron-add.description: Walking-skeleton scope (Q&A 2026-05-14 — producer=Variant2, producer-type=Variant1, mvp=Variant1, delivery=Variant1). (1) New scheduler/cron_user.py (M-SCHEDULER-CRON-USER) — mirrors firing.py shape: module-level _ctx registry installed by set_cron_user_context(scheduler, queue, jobs_session_maker); create_cron_user_job(owner_telegram_id, chat_id, recurrence:Recurrence, command:str, user_tz:str, wiki_id:str|None) INSERTs jobs.Job(kind='cron_user', status='scheduled', priority=Lane.CRON_WRITE, payload=CronUserPayload(...)) then scheduler.add_job(fire_cron_user_job, CronTrigger(**recurrence.to_cron(), timezone=user_tz), args=[job_id], id=f'cron_user:{job_id}', replace_existing=True); commit-before-add_job; returns job_id; fire_cron_user_job(job_id:int) is the picklable APScheduler callback — loads Job, guards status=='scheduled' (idempotent replay), builds CronUserQueueMsg, queue.put(Lane.CRON_WRITE, msg), UPDATE status='queued'; CronUserContextNotInitialisedError when context unset. (2) New scheduler/consumer.py (M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER) — CronConsumer(queue, bot, claude_binary, claude_config_dir, prompt_path, jobs_session_maker, timeout_s=600, slice_name='aisw-cli.slice') with constructor-DI bot (R-1 mitigation); .run() drains in single loop (NFR-1 concurrency=1, at-most-once); per item: validate CronUserQueueMsg, UPDATE Job→'running', build argv=['systemd-run','--scope','--collect','--slice='+slice_name,'--unit=cli-{job_id}','--setenv=CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=...', '--', resolve_binary(claude_binary), *system_prompt_argv(prompt_path), command], asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(*argv, stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE, cwd=neutral_cwd(claude_config_dir), env=build_env(claude_config_dir)), asyncio.wait_for(proc.communicate(), timeout=600); TimeoutError → kill_with_sequence (D-021) + ru error; exit=0 → ChainSplitter standalone → bot.send_message * n + status='finished'; exit!=0 → "❌ Ошибка ({code}): {truncate_stderr(stderr, 512)}" + status='failed'. (3) New tg/cron_add.py (M-TG-CRON-ADD) — register_cron_add_handlers(router, get_user_tz) installs Command('cron_add') handler; handler splits CommandObject.args on first '|' (left=schedule_text, right=command); parse_recurrence(schedule_text, user_tz=await get_user_tz(owner)); RecurrenceParseResult.escalate or empty command → reply CRON_ADD_USAGE_RU + CRON_ADD_USAGE_RU_HINT; on Recurrence → create_cron_user_job → reply "✅ Запланировано (id={job_id}): {_humanize_recurrence(rec)}. Команда: {command_preview}". No interactive confirm in walking skeleton (AD-02). (4) storage/jobs/payloads.py — widen CronUserPayload in place (AD-05): kind='cron_user'; recurrence:Recurrence (was cron_expr:str); command:str (was user_text); wiki_id:str|None=None (was wiki_id:str). No Alembic migration (JSON column, zero existing rows). (5) scheduler/queue_payloads.py (new, small) — Pydantic discriminated union QueueMsg with CronUserQueueMsg(kind, job_id, owner_telegram_id, chat_id, command, correlation_id, scheduled_at_utc); future kinds add siblings without callers widening (NFR-5). (6) __main__.py wiring — after scheduler.start(): cron_user.set_cron_user_context(scheduler, queue, jobs_session_maker); CronConsumer(queue=queue, bot=bot, claude_binary=settings.claude_binary, claude_config_dir=settings.claude_config_dir, prompt_path=settings.cron_user_prompt_path, jobs_session_maker=jobs_session_maker); consumer_task = asyncio.create_task(consumer.run(), name='aisw.cron_consumer'); on shutdown: consumer_task.cancel() + await with suppress(CancelledError). (7) New prompts/cron_user.md — semver: line + minimal system prompt for cron-fired CLI invocations (free-form user command, ru reply preference). (8) tg/handlers.py build_router — call register_cron_add_handlers(router, get_user_tz=...) alongside other Command handlers. (9) GRACE: M-SCHEDULER-CRON-USER + M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER + M-TG-CRON-ADD MODULE_CONTRACTs written when source files created (Step 11 Execution); knowledge-graph.xml nodes + CrossLinks (added this step); V-M-SCHEDULER-CRON-USER / V-M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER / V-M-TG-CRON-ADD verification entries (added this step); this Phase entry. New log anchors tg.command.cron_add.{parsed,escalate,scheduled,failed,usage} + scheduler.cron_user.{scheduled,fire,fire.job_missing,fire.failed} + scheduler.consumer.{drained,exec.started,exec.done,exec.timeout,exec.failed,delivered,deliver_failed,unexpected}. No new dependency, no DB migration. OUT-of-scope (follow-up bds): /cron_list, /cron_delete, /cron_edit, retry/backoff/timeout policy, DLQ wiring for cron_user, per-user rate limit, i18n, multi-process workers, bot-offline replay strategy beyond APScheduler's coalesce. Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260514-cron-add-queue-walking-skeleton-{discovery,design}.md; plan: docs/superpowers/plans/20260514-cron-add-queue-walking-skeleton-plan.md (Step 9). Unblocks the queue-consumer follow-ups previously held in aisw-cig (YAGNI-deferred there for lack of a real producer).

Phase-F.scope.NAME: aisw-o6m — adaptive scope resolution for group-2 intents (wiki_query/digest)
Phase-F.scope.description: Root-cause fix for a query-blind-search incident: wiki_ingest already has a scope mechanism (Stage-1a router + Inbox hint fast-path + hint_match.is_confident + sticky active-WIKI), but wiki_query and digest — group-2, "adaptive" intents — always ran cross-WIKI at the user root with no domain CLAUDE.md/Data-layout context, so Claude could not navigate to domain-specific paths (2026-07-02: «сколько калорий я ем» missed diet/food_log.csv even though it existed). /best-approach chose Variant 2 (phased, adapter-side, ADR-034 — no WikiRunner Protocol change needed, the adapter already has text/intent/owner_telegram_id): Phase A injects a bounded ru "Карта WIKI пользователя" layouts block (new wiki/scope.py: collect_layouts, reading each WIKI's managed CLAUDE.md zone, capped at MAX_LAYOUT_CHARS_PER_WIKI) into cross-WIKI wiki_query runs and into digest's non-primary WIKIs (extra_add_dirs) on full-digest calls only (section-expand /expand skips it — already scoped); Phase B adds resolve_query_scope, reusing the exact prod ingest fast-path thresholds (hint_match.is_confident, MIN_SCORE/MIN_MARGIN, MAX_FASTPATH_CHARS) — a confident single-domain wiki_query swaps wiki_path/wiki_id to run scoped inside that WIKI (CLAUDE.md then arrives via assemble_prompt, same as ingest), an ambiguous or unresolvable match stays cross-root with layouts injected. _WikiRunnerAdapter gained ctor params hint_catalog_resolver + owner_wikis_resolver (constructed once in main() and shared with the digest/hint-catalog wiring already built for aisw-5sd/aisw-oqq); any resolver exception degrades to legacy cross-root behaviour (FR-6, never fails the run) and logs wiki.run.scope.degraded; the happy path logs wiki.run.scope. New module M-WIKI-SCOPE (pure logic, stdlib only) + V-M-WIKI-SCOPE; M-RUNTIME-WIRING purpose/depends/annotations extended + new CrossLink to M-WIKI-SCOPE; V-M-RUNTIME-WIRING extended with tests/unit/test_main_runner_adapter.py + tests/unit/test_main_digest_adapter.py coverage and the two new markers. ADR-034. Out of scope (explicit YAGNI at the design gate): no morphological stemming in hint_match (exact token overlap only, matches the ingest fast-path's existing behaviour); no scope resolution for digest's own WIKI-selection logic (already resolved earlier via wiki_scope/_resolve_owner_wikis); no change to _ROUTABLE_INTENTS (wiki_query still bypasses the Stage-1a router by design, aisw-50z). Discovery/design/plan: docs/superpowers/specs/20260702-adaptive-scope-{discovery,design}.md; docs/superpowers/plans/20260702-adaptive-scope-plan.md.

Phase-A.NAME: aisw-xi8.A — Classifier v2 core: Intent 6-member taxonomy, WikiSlots/JobSlots, prompt 2.0.0, regression harness
Phase-A.description: Self-contained classifier-layer slice (epic aisw-xi8), first of a 6-phase decomposition (A → B → C.1 → C.2 → C.3 → C.4, strict DEPENDS ordering per Plan Sizing). (1) classifier/schema.py (M-CLASSIFIER-STAGE0): Intent narrows from the 9-member v1 enum to the closed 6-member artifact-anchored taxonomy wiki|job|web|chat|admin|unknown (FR-1); two NEW frozen Pydantic models WikiSlots(action: Literal['ingest','query','lint','catalog']|None=None) and JobSlots(action: Literal['create','cancel','list','reschedule']='create', kind: Literal['once','recurring','check_in','digest']='once', time_expr/schedule_expr/text/needle: str='') (DEC-4); NEW parse_slots(model, distilled_payload) lenient boundary parser — unknown keys ignored, ValidationError → default instance + classifier.slots.invalid log anchor, never raises (Fail-Fast-with-graceful-default, DEC-4). ClassifierResult shape unchanged (FR-1); stage0.py's unknown-fallback + retry policy (aisw-l3h) untouched. (2) prompts/classifier.md: productionise the verified draft (/home/bgs/.claude/jobs/226e4379/tmp/classifier_minimal.md) into the repo file at semver 2.0.0 + CHANGELOG entry (FR-14, DEC-12) — explicit verbatim-language rule for ALL free-text slots (FR-12), wiki/catalog worked example (fixes the measured 99/100 miss), per-intent distilled_payload contract section, chat-trap negative list + the canonical recurring-negative kept from the draft (FR-9). (3) tests/helpers: shared factory make_classifier_result(intent, action=None, kind=None, confidence=0.95, **slots) so every downstream phase (B/C) and the ~20 mechanical test-migration files construct v2 ClassifierResults from one place (DEC-14). (4) Regression harness (NEW M-CLASSIFIER-REGRESS, resolved Q4/DEC-13): tests/corpus/classifier/questions.json (≥100 labelled cases incl. the 7 measured-broken clusters — recurring/check-in swallowed by digest #13/#43/#54/#57/#71, no job management #89/#90, chat-trap losses #50/#53, fragile digest-control #35/#91/#99, sub-threshold reminder falling into the root runner #78/#96) + scripts/classifier_regress.py (async bounded-concurrency=5 over the REAL ClaudeCliBackend, per-cluster report, exit≠0 on gate failure: intent accuracy 100% honouring accept-lists, intent+action+kind ≥99%); new Makefile target `classifier-regress` — a MANDATORY MANUAL gate before any prompts/classifier.md commit, deliberately NOT wired into total-test/CI (100 real Haiku calls per run); documented in the prompt's own CHANGELOG discipline. GRACE: M-CLASSIFIER-STAGE0 contract updated in place (STATUS stays done); NEW M-CLASSIFIER-REGRESS node + V-M-CLASSIFIER-REGRESS. New RED-first suites: schema slots (valid/invalid/None-action), harness selftest (fixture corpus, non-network). No storage/scheduler/pipeline code touched — independently reviewable and mergeable ahead of B/C. Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260703-classifier-v2-{discovery,design}.md.

Phase-B.NAME: aisw-xi8.B — Scheduler/storage layer: RecurringReminderPayload/CheckInPayload, fire_recurring_job (3-strike), check_in cron+consumer bridge, NEW scheduler/manage.py job management
Phase-B.description: Bottom-layer slice (epic aisw-xi8, Phase B of 6; depends on Phase-A for the Intent/slots vocabulary referenced in docs only — no runtime import of classifier/schema.py from this layer). (1) storage/jobs/payloads.py (M-STORAGE-JOBS, resolved Q2/DEC-6): two ADDITIVE union members — RecurringReminderPayload(kind='recurring_reminder', message:str, recurrence:Recurrence, category:Literal['medication','event','generic']='generic') and CheckInPayload(kind='check_in', question_topic:str, recurrence:Recurrence, wiki_id:str|None=None); no Alembic migration (JSON column, additive discriminator tag, pydantic-verified); existing 5 kinds untouched (FR-15). (2) scheduler/firing.py (M-SCHEDULER-FIRING, DEC-7): NEW create_recurring_job (INSERT+commit jobs.Job(kind='recurring_reminder') then CronTrigger, id=f'recurring:{id}') + fire_recurring_job (picklable int callback: plain TgSender.send_message(payload.message) verbatim, NO Claude call — NFR-2; NO terminal status transition, job stays enabled; 3 consecutive send failures → disable + move_to_dlq + remove_job, digest 3-strike precedent); fire_job's once-semantics deliberately NOT generalized (three similar lines > premature abstraction). (3) scheduler/cron_user.py (M-SCHEDULER-CRON-USER, DEC-8): NEW create_check_in_job mirroring create_cron_user_job (Job row committed BEFORE add_job, CronTrigger id=f'check_in:{id}') + fire_check_in_job pushing a NEW CheckInQueueMsg onto Lane.CRON_WRITE. (4) scheduler/queue_payloads.py (M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER): CheckInQueueMsg additive QueueMsg union member (kind='check_in', job_id, owner_telegram_id, chat_id, question_topic, correlation_id, scheduled_at_utc) — the discriminator was explicitly reserved for future kinds (NFR-5). (5) scheduler/consumer.py (M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER, DEC-8): NEW per-kind branch — CLI run generating ONE ru question about question_topic via NEW prompts/check_in.md; on exit≠0/timeout → deterministic ru fallback "Хотел спросить: {question_topic}" sent verbatim (FR-6, new anchor scheduler.check_in.fallback) — never silence. (6) inbox/hint_match.py (M-INBOX): one-line promotion of the formerly-private _tokens → a public tokens() helper (DEC-9); existing hint_match tests keep passing unchanged. (7) NEW src/ai_steward_wiki/scheduler/manage.py (M-SCHEDULER-MANAGE, DEC-9) — pure functions over an injected AsyncSession + AsyncIOScheduler, no module-level context registry (called synchronously from the tg request path, not a picklable APScheduler callback): list_owner_jobs (enabled user-facing jobs — reminder_job|recurring_reminder|check_in|digest|cron_user; purge/wiki_run excluded — rendered via humanize_recurrence/local time), match_jobs_by_needle (casefold whole-token overlap scoring reusing the promoted tokens() helper), cancel_job (scheduler.remove_job + Job.status='cancelled'), reschedule_once (scheduler.reschedule_job + DateTrigger, resolved Q3), reschedule_recurring (scheduler.reschedule_job + CronTrigger + payload.recurrence rewrite, resolved Q3 — closes the measured #35/#91/#99 digest-control defect cluster), _job_key(kind,id) as the single SSoT for the reminder:|recurring:|check_in:|digest:|cron_user: job-id-string registry matching the existing literals in firing.py/cron_user.py. GRACE: M-STORAGE-JOBS/M-SCHEDULER-FIRING/M-SCHEDULER-CRON-USER/M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER/M-INBOX contracts updated in place; NEW M-SCHEDULER-MANAGE node + V-M-SCHEDULER-MANAGE; new anchors scheduler.recurring.{scheduled,fired,delivered,deliver_failed,auto_disabled}, scheduler.check_in.{scheduled,fired,fallback}. New RED-first suites: payload round-trips, recurring firing incl. 3-strike, check_in enqueue + consumer fallback, manage.py (list/needle/cancel/reschedule×2). No tg/pipeline.py or __main__.py touched yet — Phase-C wires the caller side. Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260703-classifier-v2-{discovery,design}.md.

Phase-C.1.NAME: aisw-xi8.C.1 — Pipeline dispatch spine: flat 6-intent switch, sub-threshold gate, routable predicate, WikiRunner/StreamingDelivery Protocol widening, __main__ adapter re-anchor
Phase-C.1.description: First tg-layer slice (epic aisw-xi8, Phase C.1 of 4; depends on Phase-A for Intent v2 + parse_slots, and structurally precedes C.2/C.3/C.4 which extend the JOB branch this phase only stubs). tg/pipeline.py (M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER): _run_text_pipeline becomes a flat ordered if/elif over the 6 v2 intents, each branch a thin delegation to a per-intent handler method (_handle_wiki/_handle_job/_handle_web/_handle_chat/_handle_admin/_handle_unknown — dispatch-table dict rejected as over-abstraction for 6 static branches, fully-inline blocks rejected given the file's existing ~2900 lines, DEC-1); _handle_job in THIS phase only dispatches to a stub that Phase-C.2/C.3 fill in. Immediately after tg.pipeline.classify.done, a SUBTHRESHOLD gate (CLASSIFIER_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD renamed from REMINDER_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, module const 0.85, NFR-7) fires a ru clarification + returns for confidence<threshold on intent∈{JOB,ADMIN} — new anchor tg.pipeline.subthreshold.clarify; structural double-guarantee that JOB has no fall-through to the generic root runner at all (kills defect class #78/#96 by construction, DEC-2/FR-10). The classifying Python forks are DELETED: _RECURRING_KEYWORDS + its punt (pipeline.py:415/:1562), _DIGEST_DISABLE_RE/_DIGEST_RESCHEDULE_RE/_detect_digest_action (pipeline.py:454-480) — FR-2; dateparser/parse_recurrence/_extract_hhmm/_extract_lead_minutes survive unchanged as PARAMETER validators over classifier slots, never intent-deciders (FR-3). The _ROUTABLE_INTENTS frozenset becomes a predicate function — routable ⇔ UNKNOWN, or WIKI with action∈{ingest,catalog} or action missing (DEC-3); the hint fast-path is additionally gated on action=='ingest' (catalog/None → straight to the Sonnet router, conservative — a catalog request has no content to keyword-match); wiki/catalog thus reuses the existing Stage-1a RouterIntent.LIST_WIKIS path with zero router changes (FR-11); wiki/query and wiki/lint go to the generic answer runner (streaming tail). WikiRunner.run (pipeline.py:704) and StreamingDelivery.run_and_deliver (pipeline.py:725) Protocols gain a defaulted `action: str | None = None` param (DEC-5 — deliberate ADR-034/aisw-o6m deviation; a NEW ADR is written at Step 8/13 recording it; alternative of encoding action into the Intent enum rejected as recreating the deleted taxonomy). __main__.py (M-RUNTIME-WIRING): _WikiRunnerAdapter.run's scope-resolution gate re-anchors from `intent is Intent.WIKI_QUERY` to `(intent is Intent.WIKI and action == "query")`, and the WebSearch-config gate from `intent is Intent.WEB_TASK` to `intent is Intent.WEB` (pipeline.py:460/:501/:1374 in the pre-aisw-xi8 numbering) — all other adapter mechanics (degrade-on-error, wiki.scope.collect_layouts injection) untouched. GRACE: M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER/M-TG-PIPELINE-STREAMING/M-RUNTIME-WIRING contracts updated in place; V-M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER/V-M-RUNTIME-WIRING extended. New RED-first suites: sub-threshold clarify (job/admin below/above threshold), catalog routable path (wiki/catalog with action=None → router, not admin — closes FR-11's measured miss), protocol-widening back-compat (an adapter/runner double constructed without the new param still behaves like today — action defaults to None). Test-file impact in THIS phase is limited to test_pipeline_router.py and test_main_runner_adapter.py's dispatch-shape assertions; the bulk ~20-file mechanical rename is Phase-C.4. Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260703-classifier-v2-{discovery,design}.md.

Phase-C.2.NAME: aisw-xi8.C.2 — Job management surface: list/cancel/reschedule + needle disambiguation + destructive confirm
Phase-C.2.description: Second tg-layer slice (epic aisw-xi8, Phase C.2 of 4; depends on Phase-B's scheduler/manage.py and Phase-C.1's dispatch spine). tg/pipeline.py (M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER): _handle_job now dispatches JobSlots.action=list/cancel/reschedule to NEW _handle_job_list/_handle_job_cancel/_handle_job_reschedule (create is Phase-C.3's concern). _handle_job_list calls scheduler.manage.list_owner_jobs → ru-rendered schedule list (tg.pipeline.job.list). _handle_job_cancel/_handle_job_reschedule call list_owner_jobs + match_jobs_by_needle(needle) (DEC-9): exactly 1 match → a NEW destructive 'job_cancel' category Confirm/Cancel draft (FR-8) or (for reschedule) validate time_expr/schedule_expr via parse_time/parse_recurrence first, then a confirm draft; >1 match → a NEW 'job_pick' category numbered one-column inline keyboard (jobpick:<pending_id>:<idx>, wikipick precedent confirm.py:150-175 — the pending draft carries the pending action cancel|reschedule, candidate job ids, and any pre-parsed new schedule) — tg.pipeline.job.pick_requested; 0 matches → ru "not found" + the rendered list, NO pending row — tg.pipeline.job.not_found (DEC-10). NEW _handle_job_confirm on_confirm_callback branch shared by categories job_cancel/job_pick (disambiguated by draft.category, one anchor family over 4 near-duplicate handlers — KISS/SSoT): confirmed → scheduler.manage.cancel_job / reschedule_once / reschedule_recurring + ru ack (tg.pipeline.job.cancel / tg.pipeline.job.reschedule); cancel/stale → ru notice. tapping a job_pick numbered button replays the same cancel/reschedule path for the selected job. GRACE: M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER contract updated in place; V-M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER extended; new anchors tg.pipeline.job.{list,cancel,reschedule,pick_requested,not_found} + tg.pipeline.job.confirm_{requested,created,cancelled,stale}. New RED-first suites: job list/cancel/reschedule×2 (once+recurring) + needle disambiguation (0/1/>1 matches) + confirm (job_cancel destructive gate, job_pick numbered keyboard, cancel/stale paths). Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260703-classifier-v2-{discovery,design}.md.

Phase-C.3.NAME: aisw-xi8.C.3 — Create-confirm flows for recurring/check_in + once/digest entry rewiring
Phase-C.3.description: Third tg-layer slice (epic aisw-xi8, Phase C.3 of 4; depends on Phase-B's create_recurring_job/create_check_in_job and Phase-C.1's dispatch spine). tg/pipeline.py (M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER, DEC-11): NEW _handle_job_create dispatches on JobSlots.kind — kind=once and kind=digest REUSE the existing reminder/digest confirm-draft builders and on_confirm_callback handlers completely unchanged (only the ENTRY point moves from regex-detected intent to classifier slots, FR-4/FR-7; time_expr/schedule_expr feed the existing validators where present, raw-text fallback kept per aisw-2mg NFR-2 precedent); kind=recurring and kind=check_in each get a NEW explicit confirm mirroring reminder/digest — parse schedule_expr via parse_recurrence (validator, not classifier), recap via humanize_recurrence, NEW categories 'job_recurring'/'job_checkin'. _handle_job_confirm (extended from C.2) gains the job_recurring/job_checkin branches: confirmed → scheduler.firing.create_recurring_job / scheduler.cron_user.create_check_in_job + ru ack; cancel/stale → ru notice — same shared anchor family as C.2 (tg.pipeline.job.confirm_{requested,created,cancelled,stale}, disambiguated by category). CHAT (ex-SMALLTALK, renamed) and ADMIN (ACK_ADMIN_RU) branches are re-homed into _handle_chat/_handle_admin with unchanged behaviour (FR-17); UNKNOWN → router unchanged. GRACE: M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER contract updated in place; V-M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER extended. New RED-first suites: job/create kind=recurring confirm + create_recurring_job dispatch, job/create kind=check_in confirm + create_check_in_job dispatch, kind=once/digest entry-rewiring regression (byte-identical downstream behaviour via the new slots-based entry). Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260703-classifier-v2-{discovery,design}.md.

Phase-C.4.NAME: aisw-xi8.C.4 — Test migration: mechanical intent-taxonomy rename across ~20 pinned files, obsolete-test deletion
Phase-C.4.description: Closing slice (epic aisw-xi8, Phase C.4 of 4; depends on Phases A/B/C.1/C.2/C.3 all landing first so the mapping table below is exercised against final code, not a moving target). DEC-14 mechanical mapping applied via the Phase-A factory make_classifier_result(intent, action=None, kind=None, ...) across ~20 pinned files (measured breaking surface): tests/unit/classifier/ (4 files incl. test_cli_envelope.py, test_fake_runner.py), tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline*.py (~14 files, test_pipeline_router.py the heaviest at 13 Intent-references), tests/unit/test_main_runner_adapter.py (5 references), tests/integration/classifier/test_real_cli.py. Mapping table: reminder→(job,create,once); digest→(job,create,digest); wiki_ingest→(wiki,ingest,-); wiki_query→(wiki,query,-); wiki_lint→(wiki,lint,-); web_task→(web,-,-); smalltalk→(chat,-,-); admin→(admin,-,-); unknown→(unknown,-,-). OBSOLETE tests deleted with their code: _detect_digest_action/_DIGEST_*_RE tests, _RECURRING_KEYWORDS punt tests, REMINDER_RECURRING_RU "not yet" tests (all removed in C.1, their tests should already be gone by construction — this phase is a final sweep + grace lint / mypy --strict / ruff verification that nothing references the deleted symbols). Grep false-positives explicitly NOT touched: scheduler/storage tests using "digest"/"reminder_job" as STORAGE kinds (not classifier intents), auth tests using "admin" as a ROLE literal, migration tests using ai-steward planner categories. GRACE: this Phase entry is the closing verification gate for the whole aisw-xi8 feature — `make total-test` green (NFR-1), `make classifier-regress` run once manually against the final prompts/classifier.md (FR-13, mandatory pre-commit gate per DEC-13, NOT part of total-test), grace lint 0 issues, mypy --strict clean, coverage ≥80% core. No new production code in this phase — pure test-suite migration + final gate. Discovery/design: docs/superpowers/specs/20260703-classifier-v2-{discovery,design}.md.
